policies.title: Duplicate charge refund verification
policies.source: Billing Policy Manual > Refunds > Duplicate Charges
policies.summary: Duplicate charges can be refunded after payment records are verified. Do not promise a refund before verification is complete.
guidance: Acknowledge the customer's concern and urgency.
State that the team will verify payment records before confirming the refund.
If the duplicate charge is confirmed, say the extra transaction will be refunded.
Do not say the refund has already been processed unless a payment system confirms it.
escalation: Escalate to billing operations when finance-close timing or duplicate renewal charges are mentioned.
policies.citation: Refunds require payment verification before a customer-facing commitment is made.

policies.title: Customer incident escalation response
policies.summary: For angry outage or incident complaints, acknowledge disruption, route to operations, provide a follow-up path, and avoid legal admissions.
guidance: Acknowledge the operational impact in plain language.
Escalate to operations or a support lead for incident timeline review.
Commit to a specific follow-up path, not a generic apology.
Do not admit legal liability, negligence, breach, or fault.
escalation: Escalate urgent angry complaints to operations immediately.
Mention incident timeline review when the customer asks what happened.
policies.citation: Incident responses should escalate to operations and avoid legal admissions.

policies.title: Safe automation lead qualification
policies.summary: AI automation leads should be routed to discovery. Emphasize human approval, integration mapping, and a controlled pilot before broader rollout.
guidance: Confirm that human approval before send is the recommended rollout pattern.
Ask to map current inbox sources, routing rules, escalation paths, and approval owners.
Recommend a short discovery call before implementation.
Avoid promising pricing or implementation dates without scoping.
escalation: Route qualified automation leads to a discovery call owner.
policies.citation: Qualified automation leads should move to discovery before pricing or implementation commitments.

policies.title: Technical support triage
policies.summary: Technical support replies should collect enough diagnostic context to route the issue without overpromising a fix.
guidance: Ask for account, browser, timestamp, reproduction steps, and screenshots when useful.
Summarize the issue back to the customer.
Route to technical support when product behavior or dashboard access is involved.
Do not promise a fix before engineering validates the issue.
escalation: Escalate to technical support when account access, dashboard availability, or repeated failures are mentioned.
policies.citation: Technical triage should collect diagnostics before promising a fix.

policies.title: Human approval before customer delivery
policies.source: AI Operations Policy > Customer Communications > Approval Gate
policies.summary: AI-generated customer replies must remain in human review until an authorized reviewer approves the final response.
guidance: The AI may classify, summarize, and draft.
The AI must not send directly to customers.
A human reviewer must approve the final response before live email delivery.
Staged delivery is acceptable when the email provider is not configured.
escalation: Route high-risk drafts to manual review before approval.
policies.citation: AI-generated customer messages require human approval before delivery.
